pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything we do—every single thing—is basically just a tiny blip that doesn't even register in the grand scheme of things. like, the universe is so vast and indifferent, and eventually everything just goes dark anyway. it feels kind of stupid to even try, doesn't it? to keep struggling through all this noise when it literally doesn't matter in the end.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still typing this. everything just... collapsed. the lab results, the funding, the last three years of my life. it feels like i'm just a hollow shell of a person now. i'm just so tired of fighting for a future that doesn't even want me. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to not exist for a while.

pool.baseline: The candles flicker low, and the silence of this manor is more deafening than any storm. He is gone, and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look at the moonlight on the floor and feel nothing but a cold, hollow emptiness. It feels as though my very existence is a ghost haunting a house that no longer wants me. Is there truly any dignity left in a life that has become nothing more than a slow, agonizing decay?
